framework.name: CIS Critical Security Controls v8.1
framework.description: The Center for Internet Security Critical Security Controls v8.1: a prioritized set of 18 controls and 153 safeguards defining essential cyber hygiene to defend against the most common attacks.

requirements.name: 1.1 — Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Maintain an accurate, current inventory of all enterprise assets that store or process data, recording network address, hardware address, owner, and approval status.

requirements.name: 1.2 — Address Unauthorized Assets
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Detect assets connected to the network that are not authorized and ensure they are removed, denied access, or quarantined in a timely manner.

requirements.name: 1.3 — Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Use active discovery tooling to identify connected assets and keep the enterprise asset inventory continuously updated.

requirements.name: 1.4 — Use DHCP Logging to Update Enterprise Asset Inventory
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Leverage DHCP server logging to help discover assets and keep the asset inventory current.

requirements.name: 1.5 — Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Employ passive discovery tooling to detect assets on the network and update the inventory.

requirements.name: 2.1 — Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Maintain a current inventory of all licensed software installed and running on enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 2.2 — Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Confirm that inventoried software is still vendor-supported and not past its end-of-life date.

requirements.name: 2.3 — Address Unauthorized Software
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Identify software that is unauthorized and ensure it is removed or formally documented as an exception.

requirements.name: 2.4 — Utilize Automated Software Inventory Tools
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Use automated tooling to keep the software inventory accurate, complete, and up to date.

requirements.name: 2.5 — Allowlist Authorized Software
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Use application allowlisting so that only explicitly approved software is permitted to execute.

requirements.name: 2.6 — Allowlist Authorized Libraries
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Restrict execution to approved code libraries by allowlisting permitted libraries.

requirements.name: 2.7 — Allowlist Authorized Scripts
requirements.category: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
requirements.description: Restrict the execution of scripts to those that are explicitly authorized.

requirements.name: 3.1 — Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
requirements.description: Define and maintain a documented process governing how data is handled across its full lifecycle.

requirements.name: 3.2 — Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of sensitive data noting where it resides and how sensitive it is.

requirements.name: 3.3 — Configure Data Access Control Lists
requirements.description: Apply access control lists so data is reachable only by users and services that require it.

requirements.name: 3.4 — Enforce Data Retention
requirements.description: Enforce defined minimum and maximum retention periods for stored data.

requirements.name: 3.5 — Securely Dispose of Data
requirements.description: Dispose of data securely in accordance with its sensitivity and retention requirements.

requirements.name: 3.6 — Encrypt Data on End-User Devices
requirements.description: Encrypt data stored on end-user devices to protect it if a device is lost or stolen.

requirements.name: 3.7 — Establish and Maintain a Data Classification Scheme
requirements.description: Establish a scheme that classifies data according to its sensitivity.

requirements.name: 3.8 — Document Data Flows
requirements.description: Document how data flows through systems and across organizational boundaries.

requirements.name: 3.9 — Encrypt Data on Removable Media
requirements.description: Encrypt data written to removable media.

requirements.name: 3.10 — Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
requirements.description: Encrypt sensitive data while it is being transmitted over networks.

requirements.name: 3.11 — Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
requirements.description: Encrypt sensitive data while it is stored at rest.

requirements.name: 3.12 — Segment Data Processing and Storage Based on Sensitivity
requirements.description: Separate the processing and storage of data based on its sensitivity level.

requirements.name: 3.13 — Deploy a Data Loss Prevention Solution
requirements.description: Deploy data loss prevention tooling to detect and block unauthorized data exfiltration.

requirements.name: 3.14 — Log Sensitive Data Access
requirements.description: Log access to sensitive data to support monitoring and accountability.

requirements.name: 4.1 — Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented secure configuration baseline process for assets and software.

requirements.name: 4.2 — Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Establish and maintain documented secure configuration standards for network infrastructure devices.

requirements.name: 4.3 — Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Automatically lock sessions on assets after a defined period of user inactivity.

requirements.name: 4.4 — Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Deploy and manage host-based firewalls or filtering on servers.

requirements.name: 4.5 — Implement and Manage a Firewall on End-User Devices
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Deploy and manage host-based firewalls on end-user devices with a default-deny posture.

requirements.name: 4.6 — Securely Manage Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Manage assets and software securely using approved tools, secure protocols, and controlled access.

requirements.name: 4.7 — Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Change, disable, or remove default accounts and credentials on assets and software.

requirements.name: 4.8 — Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Remove or disable services, features, and components that are not required.

requirements.name: 4.9 — Configure Trusted DNS Servers on Enterprise Assets
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Configure assets to use trusted, enterprise-controlled DNS servers.

requirements.name: 4.10 — Enforce Automatic Device Lockout on Portable End-User Devices
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Configure automatic lockout after a defined number of failed authentication attempts on portable devices.

requirements.name: 4.11 — Enforce Remote Wipe Capability on Portable End-User Devices
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Enable remote wipe capability for portable end-user devices that are lost, stolen, or retired.

requirements.name: 4.12 — Separate Enterprise Workspaces on Mobile End-User Devices
requirements.category: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
requirements.description: Maintain separate enterprise and personal workspaces on mobile end-user devices.

requirements.name: 5.1 — Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of all user, administrator, and service accounts.

requirements.name: 5.2 — Use Unique Passwords
requirements.description: Ensure every account uses a unique, sufficiently strong password.

requirements.name: 5.3 — Disable Dormant Accounts
requirements.description: Disable accounts that have been inactive beyond a defined period.

requirements.name: 5.4 — Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
requirements.description: Limit administrative privileges to dedicated administrator-only accounts separate from daily-use accounts.

requirements.name: 5.5 — Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Accounts
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of service accounts including their owner and intended purpose.

requirements.name: 5.6 — Centralize Account Management
requirements.description: Centralize account lifecycle management through a directory or identity service.

requirements.name: 6.1 — Establish an Access Granting Process
requirements.description: Define a process to grant access rights when users are hired or change roles.

requirements.name: 6.2 — Establish an Access Revoking Process
requirements.description: Define a process to promptly revoke access upon termination or role change.

requirements.name: 6.3 — Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
requirements.description: Require multi-factor authentication for externally exposed enterprise applications.

requirements.name: 6.4 — Require MFA for Remote Network Access
requirements.description: Require multi-factor authentication for remote network access.

requirements.name: 6.5 — Require MFA for Administrative Access
requirements.description: Require multi-factor authentication for all administrative access.

requirements.name: 6.6 — Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Authentication and Authorization Systems
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of the systems that provide authentication and authorization.

requirements.name: 6.7 — Centralize Access Control
requirements.description: Centralize access control through a directory service or single sign-on provider.

requirements.name: 6.8 — Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
requirements.description: Define and maintain role-based access control aligned with least-privilege principles.

requirements.name: 7.1 — Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented vulnerability management process.

requirements.name: 7.2 — Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a risk-based process for remediating identified vulnerabilities.

requirements.name: 7.3 — Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
requirements.description: Automate operating system patching on a regular, defined cadence.

requirements.name: 7.4 — Perform Automated Application Patch Management
requirements.description: Automate application patching on a regular, defined cadence.

requirements.name: 7.5 — Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Run automated, authenticated vulnerability scans of internal enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 7.6 — Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Externally-Exposed Enterprise Assets
requirements.description: Run automated vulnerability scans of externally exposed enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 7.7 — Remediate Detected Vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Remediate detected vulnerabilities according to the remediation process and prioritization.

requirements.name: 8.1 — Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented audit log management process.

requirements.name: 8.2 — Collect Audit Logs
requirements.description: Collect audit logs across enterprise assets in accordance with the logging process.

requirements.name: 8.3 — Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
requirements.description: Provision sufficient storage to retain the audit logs that are collected.

requirements.name: 8.4 — Standardize Time Synchronization
requirements.description: Synchronize clocks across assets to a consistent, trusted time source.

requirements.name: 8.5 — Collect Detailed Audit Logs
requirements.description: Capture detailed log fields such as event source, timestamp, user, and destination.

requirements.name: 8.6 — Collect DNS Query Audit Logs
requirements.description: Collect DNS query audit logs from enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 8.7 — Collect URL Request Audit Logs
requirements.description: Collect URL request audit logs from enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 8.8 — Collect Command-Line Audit Logs
requirements.description: Collect command-line activity audit logs.

requirements.name: 8.9 — Centralize Audit Logs
requirements.description: Aggregate audit logs into a central location for correlation, analysis, and retention.

requirements.name: 8.10 — Retain Audit Logs
requirements.description: Retain audit logs for at least a defined minimum period.

requirements.name: 8.11 — Conduct Audit Log Reviews
requirements.description: Regularly review audit logs to detect anomalies and potential threats.

requirements.name: 8.12 — Collect Service Provider Logs
requirements.description: Collect logs from service providers where the capability is supported.

requirements.name: 9.1 — Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
requirements.description: Use only web browsers and email clients that remain fully vendor-supported.

requirements.name: 9.2 — Use DNS Filtering Services
requirements.description: Use DNS filtering services to block access to known malicious domains.

requirements.name: 9.3 — Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
requirements.description: Enforce network-based URL filtering to limit access to harmful or unauthorized sites.

requirements.name: 9.4 — Restrict Unnecessary or Unauthorized Browser and Email Client Extensions
requirements.description: Restrict and remove unauthorized browser and email client add-ons and extensions.

requirements.name: 9.5 — Implement DMARC
requirements.description: Implement DMARC together with SPF and DKIM to reduce email spoofing.

requirements.name: 9.6 — Block Unnecessary File Types
requirements.description: Block unnecessary or high-risk file types at the email gateway.

requirements.name: 9.7 — Deploy and Maintain Email Server Anti-Malware Protections
requirements.description: Deploy and maintain anti-malware scanning protections on email servers.

requirements.name: 10.1 — Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
requirements.description: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software on enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 10.2 — Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
requirements.description: Configure anti-malware signatures and definitions to update automatically.

requirements.name: 10.3 — Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
requirements.description: Disable autorun and autoplay features for removable media.

requirements.name: 10.4 — Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
requirements.description: Automatically scan removable media for malware when it is connected.

requirements.name: 10.5 — Enable Anti-Exploitation Features
requirements.description: Enable anti-exploitation protections such as DEP and ASLR where available.

requirements.name: 10.6 — Centrally Manage Anti-Malware Software
requirements.description: Centrally manage anti-malware software configuration and reporting.

requirements.name: 10.7 — Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software
requirements.description: Use anti-malware software capable of behavior-based detection.

requirements.name: 11.1 — Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented data recovery process.

requirements.name: 11.2 — Perform Automated Backups
requirements.description: Perform automated, regularly scheduled backups of in-scope enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 11.3 — Protect Recovery Data
requirements.description: Protect backup and recovery data with controls equivalent to the source data.

requirements.name: 11.4 — Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
requirements.description: Maintain at least one recovery copy that is isolated from the production network.

requirements.name: 11.5 — Test Data Recovery
requirements.description: Periodically test restoration of data from backups to confirm recoverability.

requirements.name: 12.1 — Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
requirements.description: Keep network infrastructure firmware and software current and vendor-supported.

requirements.name: 12.2 — Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
requirements.description: Design and maintain a secure, segmented network architecture.

requirements.name: 12.3 — Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
requirements.description: Manage network infrastructure securely using controlled and authenticated methods.

requirements.name: 12.4 — Establish and Maintain Architecture Diagram(s)
requirements.description: Maintain up-to-date network architecture diagrams.

requirements.name: 12.5 — Centralize Network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA)
requirements.description: Centralize network authentication, authorization, and accounting functions.

requirements.name: 12.6 — Use of Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols
requirements.description: Use secure protocols for network device management and communications.

requirements.name: 12.7 — Ensure Remote Devices Utilize a VPN and Connect to Enterprise AAA
requirements.description: Require remote devices to connect through a VPN tied to the enterprise AAA infrastructure.

requirements.name: 12.8 — Establish and Maintain Dedicated Computing Resources for All Administrative Work
requirements.description: Use dedicated, hardened computing resources for administrative tasks.

requirements.name: 13.1 — Centralize Security Event Alerting
requirements.description: Centralize security event alerting across assets to enable correlation and analysis.

requirements.name: 13.2 — Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Detection Solution
requirements.description: Deploy host-based intrusion detection on enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 13.3 — Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
requirements.description: Deploy network intrusion detection sensors at appropriate points.

requirements.name: 13.4 — Perform Traffic Filtering Between Network Segments
requirements.description: Filter traffic between network segments based on business need.

requirements.name: 13.5 — Manage Access Control for Remote Assets
requirements.description: Control network access for remote assets based on posture and policy.

requirements.name: 13.6 — Collect Network Traffic Flow Logs
requirements.description: Collect network traffic flow logs for monitoring and investigation.

requirements.name: 13.7 — Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Prevention Solution
requirements.description: Deploy host-based intrusion prevention on enterprise assets.

requirements.name: 13.8 — Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution
requirements.description: Deploy network intrusion prevention capabilities.

requirements.name: 13.9 — Deploy Port-Level Access Control
requirements.description: Implement port-level access control such as 802.1x or certificate-based authentication.

requirements.name: 13.10 — Perform Application Layer Filtering
requirements.description: Perform filtering of traffic at the application layer.

requirements.name: 13.11 — Tune Security Event Alerting Thresholds
requirements.description: Tune security event alerting thresholds to reduce noise and improve detection fidelity.

requirements.name: 14.1 — Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
requirements.description: Establish and maintain an ongoing security awareness program for the workforce.

requirements.name: 14.2 — Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
requirements.description: Train staff to recognize social engineering and phishing attacks.

requirements.name: 14.3 — Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
requirements.description: Train staff on strong authentication practices including MFA and password hygiene.

requirements.name: 14.4 — Train Workforce on Data Handling Best Practices
requirements.description: Train staff on proper handling, storage, and disposal of data.

requirements.name: 14.5 — Train Workforce Members on Causes of Unintentional Data Exposure
requirements.description: Train staff on the causes of accidental data exposure.

requirements.name: 14.6 — Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
requirements.description: Train staff to recognize and report security incidents.

requirements.name: 14.7 — Train Workforce to Identify and Report Missing Security Updates
requirements.description: Train staff to identify and report when their assets are missing security updates.

requirements.name: 14.8 — Train Workforce on Dangers of Transmitting Data Over Insecure Networks
requirements.description: Train staff on the risks of connecting to and transmitting data over insecure networks.

requirements.name: 14.9 — Conduct Role-Specific Security Awareness and Skills Training
requirements.description: Provide role-specific security awareness and skills training.

requirements.name: 15.1 — Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of service providers used by the enterprise.

requirements.name: 15.2 — Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a policy governing the management of service providers.

requirements.name: 15.3 — Classify Service Providers
requirements.description: Classify service providers by risk, criticality, or data sensitivity.

requirements.name: 15.4 — Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
requirements.description: Ensure service provider contracts incorporate appropriate security requirements.

requirements.name: 15.5 — Assess Service Providers
requirements.description: Assess service providers against the enterprise's security expectations.

requirements.name: 15.6 — Monitor Service Providers
requirements.description: Monitor service providers for ongoing compliance with security requirements.

requirements.name: 15.7 — Securely Decommission Service Providers
requirements.description: Securely decommission service providers when the relationship ends.

requirements.name: 16.1 — Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented secure software development process.

requirements.name: 16.2 — Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Establish a process to accept and remediate reported software vulnerabilities.

requirements.name: 16.3 — Perform Root Cause Analysis on Security Vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Perform root cause analysis on discovered security vulnerabilities.

requirements.name: 16.4 — Establish and Manage an Inventory of Third-Party Software Components
requirements.description: Maintain an inventory of third-party software components used in applications.

requirements.name: 16.5 — Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components
requirements.description: Use current, trusted third-party software components and avoid vulnerable ones.

requirements.name: 16.6 — Establish a Severity Rating System for Application Vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Establish a severity rating system and process for application vulnerabilities.

requirements.name: 16.7 — Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure
requirements.description: Use standardized hardened configuration templates for application infrastructure.

requirements.name: 16.8 — Separate Production and Non-Production Systems
requirements.description: Keep production and non-production systems separated.

requirements.name: 16.9 — Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
requirements.description: Train developers in application security concepts and secure coding practices.

requirements.name: 16.10 — Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures
requirements.description: Apply secure design principles within application architectures.

requirements.name: 16.11 — Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components
requirements.description: Use vetted modules or services for sensitive application security functions.

requirements.name: 16.12 — Implement Code-Level Security Checks
requirements.description: Implement code-level security analysis using static and dynamic testing.

requirements.name: 16.13 — Conduct Application Penetration Testing
requirements.description: Conduct penetration testing of applications.

requirements.name: 16.14 — Conduct Threat Modeling
requirements.description: Conduct threat modeling of applications to identify design weaknesses.

requirements.name: 17.1 — Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
requirements.description: Designate personnel responsible for managing incident handling.

requirements.name: 17.2 — Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
requirements.description: Maintain current contact information for parties involved in reporting incidents.

requirements.name: 17.3 — Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
requirements.description: Establish an enterprise-wide process for personnel to report incidents.

requirements.name: 17.4 — Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a documented incident response process.

requirements.name: 17.5 — Assign Key Roles and Responsibilities
requirements.description: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response.

requirements.name: 17.6 — Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response
requirements.description: Define communication mechanisms to be used during incident response.

requirements.name: 17.7 — Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
requirements.description: Conduct routine exercises to test the incident response capability.

requirements.name: 17.8 — Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
requirements.description: Conduct post-incident reviews to capture lessons learned and improve.

requirements.name: 17.9 — Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds
requirements.description: Establish thresholds that define what constitutes a reportable security incident.

requirements.name: 18.1 — Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program scoped to the enterprise.

requirements.name: 18.2 — Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
requirements.description: Perform periodic external penetration tests.

requirements.name: 18.3 — Remediate Penetration Test Findings
requirements.description: Remediate findings identified during penetration tests.

requirements.name: 18.4 — Validate Security Measures
requirements.description: Validate that security controls function as intended after changes or tests.

requirements.name: 18.5 — Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
requirements.description: Perform periodic internal penetration tests.
